[Tool: Write] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/refactor_github_classes.py","content":"#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Rename DryRunGitHub → DryRunLocalGitHub and PrintingGitHub → PrintingLocalGitHub.

Example:
  python refactor_github_classes.py --dry-run
  python refactor_github_classes.py
"""
import sys
from pathlib import Path
import libcst as cst
from libcst import matchers as m

class RenameGitHubClassesTransformer(cst.CSTTransformer):
    """Rename DryRunGitHub and PrintingGitHub classes to LocalGitHub variants."""

    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.changes_made = 0

    def leave_Name(self, original_node, updated_node):
        """Rename class names in all contexts (definitions, imports, usage)."""
        # Only rename exact matches (word boundaries)
        if updated_node.value == "DryRunGitHub":
            self.changes_made += 1
            return updated_node.with_changes(value="DryRunLocalGitHub")
        elif updated_node.value == "PrintingGitHub":
            self.changes_made += 1
            return updated_node.with_changes(value="PrintingLocalGitHub")

        return updated_node

def transform_file(file_path: Path, dry_run: bool = False) -> bool:
    """Transform a single file. Returns True if changes made."""
    if not file_path.exists():
        print(f"Skipping {file_path}: does not exist")
        return False

    source = file_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

    try:
        tree = cst.parse_module(source)
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Parse error in {file_path}: {e}")
        return False

    transformer = RenameGitHubClassesTransformer()
    new_tree = tree.visit(transformer)

    if transformer.changes_made == 0:
        return False

    if dry_run:
        print(f"[DRY RUN] Would modify {file_path}: {transformer.changes_made} changes")
        return True

    file_path.write_text(new_tree.code, encoding="utf-8")
    print(f"✓ Modified {file_path}: {transformer.changes_made} changes")
    return True

def main():
    dry_run = "--dry-run" in sys.argv

    # List of files to transform (from grep results)
    files = [
        "packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/dry_run.py",
        "packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/printing.py",
        "src/erk/core/context.py",
        "tests/integration/test_dryrun_integration.py",
        "packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/printing/base.py",
        "docs/learned/architecture/tripwires.md",
        "docs/learned/architecture/gateway-abc-implementation.md",
        "docs/learned/architecture/gateway-error-boundaries.md",
        "docs/learned/architecture/erk-architecture.md",
        ".claude/skills/objective/references/format.md",
    ]

    total_modified = 0
    for file_str in files:
        file_path = Path(file_str)
        if transform_file(file_path, dry_run):
            total_modified += 1

    print(f"\n{'[DRY RUN] Would modify' if dry_run else 'Modified'} {total_modified} files")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
"}
[Tool: Write] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/refactor_github_classes.py","content":"#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Rename DryRunGitHub → DryRunLocalGitHub and PrintingGitHub → PrintingLocalGitHub.

Example:
  python refactor_github_classes.py --dry-run
  python refactor_github_classes.py
"""
import sys
from pathlib import Path
import libcst as cst
from libcst import matchers as m

class RenameGitHubClassesTransformer(cst.CSTTransformer):
    """Rename DryRunGitHub and PrintingGitHub classes to LocalGitHub variants."""

    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.changes_made = 0

    def leave_Name(self, original_node, updated_node):
        """Rename class names in all contexts (definitions, imports, usage)."""
        # Only rename exact matches (word boundaries)
        if updated_node.value == "DryRunGitHub":
            self.changes_made += 1
            return updated_node.with_changes(value="DryRunLocalGitHub")
        elif updated_node.value == "PrintingGitHub":
            self.changes_made += 1
            return updated_node.with_changes(value="PrintingLocalGitHub")

        return updated_node

def transform_file(file_path: Path, dry_run: bool = False) -> bool:
    """Transform a single file. Returns True if changes made."""
    if not file_path.exists():
        print(f"Skipping {file_path}: does not exist")
        return False

    source = file_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

    try:
        tree = cst.parse_module(source)
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Parse error in {file_path}: {e}")
        return False

    transformer = RenameGitHubClassesTransformer()
    new_tree = tree.visit(transformer)

    if transformer.changes_made == 0:
        return False

    if dry_run:
        print(f"[DRY RUN] Would modify {file_path}: {transformer.changes_made} changes")
        return True

    file_path.write_text(new_tree.code, encoding="utf-8")
    print(f"✓ Modified {file_path}: {transformer.changes_made} changes")
    return True

def main():
    dry_run = "--dry-run" in sys.argv

    # List of files to transform (from grep results)
    files = [
        "packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/dry_run.py",
        "packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/printing.py",
        "src/erk/core/context.py",
        "tests/integration/test_dryrun_integration.py",
        "packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/printing/base.py",
        "docs/learned/architecture/tripwires.md",
        "docs/learned/architecture/gateway-abc-implementation.md",
        "docs/learned/architecture/gateway-error-boundaries.md",
        "docs/learned/architecture/erk-architecture.md",
        ".claude/skills/objective/references/format.md",
    ]

    total_modified = 0
    for file_str in files:
        file_path = Path(file_str)
        if transform_file(file_path, dry_run):
            total_modified += 1

    print(f"\n{'[DRY RUN] Would modify' if dry_run else 'Modified'} {total_modified} files")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
"}

- tests/integration/test_dryrun_integration.py

[Tool: Write] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/rename_real_github.py","content":"#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Rename RealGitHub to RealLocalGitHub across the codebase.

This script renames:
- Class definition: class RealGitHub → class RealLocalGitHub
- Imports: from ... import RealGitHub
- Type annotations: ... -> RealGitHub
- Constructor calls: RealGitHub(...)
- Docstrings and comments mentioning RealGitHub

It does NOT rename:
- RealGitHubIssues
- RealGitHubAdmin
- RealRemoteGitHub
- Variable names like real_github

Example:
  python rename_real_github.py packages/erk-shared/src/**/*.py --dry-run
  python rename_real_github.py packages/erk-shared/src/**/*.py
"""
import sys
from pathlib import Path
import libcst as cst
from libcst import matchers as m

class RenameRealGitHubTransformer(cst.CSTTransformer):
    """Transform RealGitHub to RealLocalGitHub."""

    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.changes_made = 0

    def leave_ClassDef(self, original_node, updated_node):
        # Rename class definition: class RealGitHub
        if m.matches(updated_node, m.ClassDef(name=m.Name("RealGitHub"))):
            self.changes_made += 1
            return updated_node.with_changes(name=cst.Name("RealLocalGitHub"))
        return updated_node

    def leave_Name(self, original_node, updated_node):
        # Rename all Name nodes with value "RealGitHub"
        # This covers:
        # - Imports: from ... import RealGitHub
        # - Type annotations: ... -> RealGitHub
        # - Constructor calls: RealGitHub(...)
        # - Type aliases, etc.
        if updated_node.value == "RealGitHub":
            self.changes_made += 1
            return updated_node.with_changes(value="RealLocalGitHub")
        return updated_node

    def leave_SimpleString(self, original_node, updated_node):
        # Rename in string literals (docstrings, comments)
        # Only rename if the string contains exactly "RealGitHub" as a word
        value = updated_node.evaluated_value
        if isinstance(value, str) and "RealGitHub" in value:
            # Use word boundary logic to avoid renaming RealGitHubIssues, etc.
            import re
            new_value = re.sub(r'\bRealGitHub\b', 'RealLocalGitHub', value)
            if new_value != value:
                self.changes_made += 1
                # Preserve the quote style
                quote = updated_node.value[0]
                # Handle triple quotes
                if updated_node.value.startswith('"""') or updated_node.value.startswith("'''"):
                    quote_style = updated_node.value[:3]
                    return updated_node.with_changes(value=f'{quote_style}{new_value}{quote_style}')
                else:
                    return updated_node.with_changes(value=f'{quote}{new_value}{quote}')
        return updated_node

    def leave_FormattedString(self, original_node, updated_node):
        # Handle f-strings - we need to check the parts
        # For simplicity in this case, we'll let Name nodes inside handle it
        return updated_node

def transform_file(file_path: Path, dry_run: bool = False) -> bool:
    """Transform a single file. Returns True if changes made."""
    if not file_path.exists():
        print(f"Skipping {file_path}: does not exist")
        return False

    source = file_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

    try:
        tree = cst.parse_module(source)
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Parse error in {file_path}: {e}")
        return False

    transformer = RenameRealGitHubTransformer()
    new_tree = tree.visit(transformer)

    if transformer.changes_made == 0:
        return False

    if dry_run:
        print(f"[DRY RUN] Would modify {file_path}: {transformer.changes_made} changes")
        return True

    file_path.write_text(new_tree.code, encoding="utf-8")
    print(f"✓ Modified {file_path}: {transformer.changes_made} changes")
    return True

def main():
    dry_run = "--dry-run" in sys.argv
    paths = [arg for arg in sys.argv[1:] if arg != "--dry-run"]

    if not paths:
        print(__doc__)
        sys.exit(1)

    total_modified = 0
    for path_str in paths:
        path = Path(path_str)
        if path.is_file():
            if transform_file(path, dry_run):
                total_modified += 1
        elif path.is_dir():
            for py_file in path.rglob("*.py"):
                if transform_file(py_file, dry_run):
                    total_modified += 1

    print(f"\n{'[DRY RUN] Would modify' if dry_run else 'Modified'} {total_modified} files")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
"}
[Tool: Write] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/rename_real_github.py","content":"#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Rename RealGitHub to RealLocalGitHub across the codebase.

This script renames:
- Class definition: class RealGitHub → class RealLocalGitHub
- Imports: from ... import RealGitHub
- Type annotations: ... -> RealGitHub
- Constructor calls: RealGitHub(...)
- Docstrings and comments mentioning RealGitHub

It does NOT rename:
- RealGitHubIssues
- RealGitHubAdmin
- RealRemoteGitHub
- Variable names like real_github

Example:
  python rename_real_github.py packages/erk-shared/src/**/*.py --dry-run
  python rename_real_github.py packages/erk-shared/src/**/*.py
"""
import sys
from pathlib import Path
import libcst as cst
from libcst import matchers as m

class RenameRealGitHubTransformer(cst.CSTTransformer):
    """Transform RealGitHub to RealLocalGitHub."""

    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.changes_made = 0

    def leave_ClassDef(self, original_node, updated_node):
        # Rename class definition: class RealGitHub
        if m.matches(updated_node, m.ClassDef(name=m.Name("RealGitHub"))):
            self.changes_made += 1
            return updated_node.with_changes(name=cst.Name("RealLocalGitHub"))
        return updated_node

    def leave_Name(self, original_node, updated_node):
        # Rename all Name nodes with value "RealGitHub"
        # This covers:
        # - Imports: from ... import RealGitHub
        # - Type annotations: ... -> RealGitHub
        # - Constructor calls: RealGitHub(...)
        # - Type aliases, etc.
        if updated_node.value == "RealGitHub":
            self.changes_made += 1
            return updated_node.with_changes(value="RealLocalGitHub")
        return updated_node

    def leave_SimpleString(self, original_node, updated_node):
        # Rename in string literals (docstrings, comments)
        # Only rename if the string contains exactly "RealGitHub" as a word
        value = updated_node.evaluated_value
        if isinstance(value, str) and "RealGitHub" in value:
            # Use word boundary logic to avoid renaming RealGitHubIssues, etc.
            import re
            new_value = re.sub(r'\bRealGitHub\b', 'RealLocalGitHub', value)
            if new_value != value:
                self.changes_made += 1
                # Preserve the quote style
                quote = updated_node.value[0]
                # Handle triple quotes
                if updated_node.value.startswith('"""') or updated_node.value.startswith("'''"):
                    quote_style = updated_node.value[:3]
                    return updated_node.with_changes(value=f'{quote_style}{new_value}{quote_style}')
                else:
                    return updated_node.with_changes(value=f'{quote}{new_value}{quote}')
        return updated_node

    def leave_FormattedString(self, original_node, updated_node):
        # Handle f-strings - we need to check the parts
        # For simplicity in this case, we'll let Name nodes inside handle it
        return updated_node

def transform_file(file_path: Path, dry_run: bool = False) -> bool:
    """Transform a single file. Returns True if changes made."""
    if not file_path.exists():
        print(f"Skipping {file_path}: does not exist")
        return False

    source = file_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

    try:
        tree = cst.parse_module(source)
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Parse error in {file_path}: {e}")
        return False

    transformer = RenameRealGitHubTransformer()
    new_tree = tree.visit(transformer)

    if transformer.changes_made == 0:
        return False

    if dry_run:
        print(f"[DRY RUN] Would modify {file_path}: {transformer.changes_made} changes")
        return True

    file_path.write_text(new_tree.code, encoding="utf-8")
    print(f"✓ Modified {file_path}: {transformer.changes_made} changes")
    return True

def main():
    dry_run = "--dry-run" in sys.argv
    paths = [arg for arg in sys.argv[1:] if arg != "--dry-run"]

    if not paths:
        print(__doc__)
        sys.exit(1)

    total_modified = 0
    for path_str in paths:
        path = Path(path_str)
        if path.is_file():
            if transform_file(path, dry_run):
                total_modified += 1
        elif path.is_dir():
            for py_file in path.rglob("*.py"):
                if transform_file(py_file, dry_run):
                    total_modified += 1

    print(f"\n{'[DRY RUN] Would modify' if dry_run else 'Modified'} {total_modified} files")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
"}

[Tool: Write] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/refactor_fake_github.py","content":"#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Rename FakeGitHub class to FakeLocalGitHub across the codebase.

IMPORTANT: Only renames the class FakeGitHub (exact match with word boundaries).
Does NOT rename: FakeGitHubIssues, FakeGitHubAdmin, FakeRemoteGitHub, or variable names.

Example:
  python refactor_fake_github.py --dry-run
  python refactor_fake_github.py
"""
import sys
from pathlib import Path
import libcst as cst
from libcst import matchers as m


class RenameFakeGitHubTransformer(cst.CSTTransformer):
    """Transform FakeGitHub to FakeLocalGitHub in all contexts."""

    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.changes_made = 0

    def leave_ClassDef(self, original_node, updated_node):
        """Rename class definition."""
        if m.matches(updated_node, m.ClassDef(name=m.Name("FakeGitHub"))):
            self.changes_made += 1
            return updated_node.with_changes(name=cst.Name("FakeLocalGitHub"))
        return updated_node

    def leave_Name(self, original_node, updated_node):
        """Rename all references to FakeGitHub (imports, type annotations, etc)."""
        if updated_node.value == "FakeGitHub":
            self.changes_made += 1
            return updated_node.with_changes(value="FakeLocalGitHub")
        return updated_node

    def leave_SimpleString(self, original_node, updated_node):
        """Rename in docstrings and string literals (but not variable names)."""
        value = updated_node.value
        quote = value[0]

        # Handle triple quotes
        if value.startswith('"""') or value.startswith("'''"):
            quote = value[:3]
            content = value[3:-3]
            if "FakeGitHub" in content:
                # Only replace exact word matches (not part of longer words)
                import re
                new_content = re.sub(r'\bFakeGitHub\b', 'FakeLocalGitHub', content)
                if new_content != content:
                    self.changes_made += 1
                    return updated_node.with_changes(value=f'{quote}{new_content}{quote}')
        # Handle single/double quotes
        elif len(value) >= 2 and value[0] in ('"', "'"):
            content = value[1:-1]
            if "FakeGitHub" in content:
                import re
                new_content = re.sub(r'\bFakeGitHub\b', 'FakeLocalGitHub', content)
                if new_content != content:
                    self.changes_made += 1
                    return updated_node.with_changes(value=f'{quote}{new_content}{quote}')

        return updated_node


def transform_file(file_path: Path, dry_run: bool = False) -> bool:
    """Transform a single file. Returns True if changes made."""
    if not file_path.exists():
        print(f"Skipping {file_path}: does not exist")
        return False

    source = file_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

    try:
        tree = cst.parse_module(source)
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Parse error in {file_path}: {e}")
        return False

    transformer = RenameFakeGitHubTransformer()
    new_tree = tree.visit(transformer)

    if transformer.changes_made == 0:
        return False

    if dry_run:
        print(f"[DRY RUN] Would modify {file_path}: {transformer.changes_made} changes")
        return True

    file_path.write_text(new_tree.code, encoding="utf-8")
    print(f"✓ Modified {file_path}: {transformer.changes_made} changes")
    return True


def main():
    dry_run = "--dry-run" in sys.argv

    # Target directories
    base = Path("/home/runner/work/erk/erk")
    target_dirs = [
        base / "packages/erk-shared/src",
        base / "src/erk",
        base / "tests",
        base / "packages/erk-statusline",
    ]

    total_modified = 0
    for target_dir in target_dirs:
        if not target_dir.exists():
            print(f"Skipping {target_dir}: does not exist")
            continue

        for py_file in target_dir.rglob("*.py"):
            if transform_file(py_file, dry_run):
                total_modified += 1

    print(f"\n{'[DRY RUN] Would modify' if dry_run else 'Modified'} {total_modified} files")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
"}
[Tool: Write] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/refactor_fake_github.py","content":"#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Rename FakeGitHub class to FakeLocalGitHub across the codebase.

IMPORTANT: Only renames the class FakeGitHub (exact match with word boundaries).
Does NOT rename: FakeGitHubIssues, FakeGitHubAdmin, FakeRemoteGitHub, or variable names.

Example:
  python refactor_fake_github.py --dry-run
  python refactor_fake_github.py
"""
import sys
from pathlib import Path
import libcst as cst
from libcst import matchers as m


class RenameFakeGitHubTransformer(cst.CSTTransformer):
    """Transform FakeGitHub to FakeLocalGitHub in all contexts."""

    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.changes_made = 0

    def leave_ClassDef(self, original_node, updated_node):
        """Rename class definition."""
        if m.matches(updated_node, m.ClassDef(name=m.Name("FakeGitHub"))):
            self.changes_made += 1
            return updated_node.with_changes(name=cst.Name("FakeLocalGitHub"))
        return updated_node

    def leave_Name(self, original_node, updated_node):
        """Rename all references to FakeGitHub (imports, type annotations, etc)."""
        if updated_node.value == "FakeGitHub":
            self.changes_made += 1
            return updated_node.with_changes(value="FakeLocalGitHub")
        return updated_node

    def leave_SimpleString(self, original_node, updated_node):
        """Rename in docstrings and string literals (but not variable names)."""
        value = updated_node.value
        quote = value[0]

        # Handle triple quotes
        if value.startswith('"""') or value.startswith("'''"):
            quote = value[:3]
            content = value[3:-3]
            if "FakeGitHub" in content:
                # Only replace exact word matches (not part of longer words)
                import re
                new_content = re.sub(r'\bFakeGitHub\b', 'FakeLocalGitHub', content)
                if new_content != content:
                    self.changes_made += 1
                    return updated_node.with_changes(value=f'{quote}{new_content}{quote}')
        # Handle single/double quotes
        elif len(value) >= 2 and value[0] in ('"', "'"):
            content = value[1:-1]
            if "FakeGitHub" in content:
                import re
                new_content = re.sub(r'\bFakeGitHub\b', 'FakeLocalGitHub', content)
                if new_content != content:
                    self.changes_made += 1
                    return updated_node.with_changes(value=f'{quote}{new_content}{quote}')

        return updated_node


def transform_file(file_path: Path, dry_run: bool = False) -> bool:
    """Transform a single file. Returns True if changes made."""
    if not file_path.exists():
        print(f"Skipping {file_path}: does not exist")
        return False

    source = file_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

    try:
        tree = cst.parse_module(source)
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Parse error in {file_path}: {e}")
        return False

    transformer = RenameFakeGitHubTransformer()
    new_tree = tree.visit(transformer)

    if transformer.changes_made == 0:
        return False

    if dry_run:
        print(f"[DRY RUN] Would modify {file_path}: {transformer.changes_made} changes")
        return True

    file_path.write_text(new_tree.code, encoding="utf-8")
    print(f"✓ Modified {file_path}: {transformer.changes_made} changes")
    return True


def main():
    dry_run = "--dry-run" in sys.argv

    # Target directories
    base = Path("/home/runner/work/erk/erk")
    target_dirs = [
        base / "packages/erk-shared/src",
        base / "src/erk",
        base / "tests",
        base / "packages/erk-statusline",
    ]

    total_modified = 0
    for target_dir in target_dirs:
        if not target_dir.exists():
            print(f"Skipping {target_dir}: does not exist")
            continue

        for py_file in target_dir.rglob("*.py"):
            if transform_file(py_file, dry_run):
                total_modified += 1

    print(f"\n{'[DRY RUN] Would modify' if dry_run else 'Modified'} {total_modified} files")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
"}

import libcst as cst
from pathlib import Path

# Read a sample file
file_path = Path('packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/fake.py')
source = file_path.read_text()

# Parse and show a snippet
tree = cst.parse_module(source)
lines = source.split('\n')
for i, line in enumerate(lines[:50], 1):
    if 'FakeGitHub' in line and 'FakeGitHubIssues' not in line and 'FakeGitHubAdmin' not in line:
        print(f'{i}: {line}')
"","description":"Check original FakeGitHub references"}
[Tool: Bash] {"command":"uv run python -c "
import libcst as cst
from pathlib import Path

# Read a sample file
file_path = Path('packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/fake.py')
source = file_path.read_text()

# Parse and show a snippet
tree = cst.parse_module(source)
lines = source.split('\n')
for i, line in enumerate(lines[:50], 1):
    if 'FakeGitHub' in line and 'FakeGitHubIssues' not in line and 'FakeGitHubAdmin' not in line:
        print(f'{i}: {line}')
"","description":"Check original FakeGitHub references"}

... [60 lines omitted] ...

[Tool: Bash] {"command":"python3 << 'EOF'
import re
from pathlib import Path

# Pattern to find FakeGitHub word boundaries, but not as part of other class names
pattern = re.compile(r'\bFakeGitHub\b(?!Issues|Admin)')

target_dirs = [
    'packages/erk-shared/src',
    'packages/erk-statusline', 
    'src/erk',
    'tests'
]

non_comment_refs = []
comment_refs = []

for target in target_dirs:
    path = Path(target)
    if not path.exists():
        continue
    for py_file in path.rglob("*.py"):
        content = py_file.read_text()
        for i, line in enumerate(content.split('\n'), 1):
            if pattern.search(line):
                # Exclude the other class names
                if any(x in line for x in ['FakeGitHubIssues', 'FakeGitHubAdmin', 'FakeRemoteGitHub']):
                    continue
                
                stripped = line.strip()
                # Check if it's a comment
                if '#' in line:
                    code_part = line.split('#')[0].strip()
                    if pattern.search(code_part):
                        non_comment_refs.append(f"{py_file}:{i}")
                    else:
                        comment_refs.append(f"{py_file}:{i}")
                else:
                    non_comment_refs.append(f"{py_file}:{i}")

print(f"✅ Non-comment references to FakeGitHub: {len(non_comment_refs)}")
print(f"📝 Comment-only references: {len(comment_refs)}")

if non_comment_refs:
    print("\n⚠️  WARNING: Found non-comment references:")
    for ref in non_comment_refs[:10]:
        print(f"  {ref}")
else:
    print("\n✅ SUCCESS: All FakeGitHub references have been renamed to FakeLocalGitHub!")
    print(f"   (Found {len(comment_refs)} comment-only references, which is OK)")
EOF
","description":"Final verification of rename completion"}
[Tool: Bash] {"command":"python3 << 'EOF'
import re
from pathlib import Path

# Pattern to find FakeGitHub word boundaries, but not as part of other class names
pattern = re.compile(r'\bFakeGitHub\b(?!Issues|Admin)')

target_dirs = [
    'packages/erk-shared/src',
    'packages/erk-statusline', 
    'src/erk',
    'tests'
]

non_comment_refs = []
comment_refs = []

for target in target_dirs:
    path = Path(target)
    if not path.exists():
        continue
    for py_file in path.rglob("*.py"):
        content = py_file.read_text()
        for i, line in enumerate(content.split('\n'), 1):
            if pattern.search(line):
                # Exclude the other class names
                if any(x in line for x in ['FakeGitHubIssues', 'FakeGitHubAdmin', 'FakeRemoteGitHub']):
                    continue
                
                stripped = line.strip()
                # Check if it's a comment
                if '#' in line:
                    code_part = line.split('#')[0].strip()
                    if pattern.search(code_part):
                        non_comment_refs.append(f"{py_file}:{i}")
                    else:
                        comment_refs.append(f"{py_file}:{i}")
                else:
                    non_comment_refs.append(f"{py_file}:{i}")

print(f"✅ Non-comment references to FakeGitHub: {len(non_comment_refs)}")
print(f"📝 Comment-only references: {len(comment_refs)}")

if non_comment_refs:
    print("\n⚠️  WARNING: Found non-comment references:")
    for ref in non_comment_refs[:10]:
        print(f"  {ref}")
else:
    print("\n✅ SUCCESS: All FakeGitHub references have been renamed to FakeLocalGitHub!")
    print(f"   (Found {len(comment_refs)} comment-only references, which is OK)")
EOF

"""Abstract base class for GitHub operations."""
from __future__ import annotations
from abc import ABC, abstractmethod from pathlib import Path from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueInfo from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import ( BodyContent, GitHubRepoLocation, IssueFilterState, MergeError, MergeResult, PRCheckRun, PRDetails, PRListState, PRNotFound, PRReviewThread, PullRequestInfo, WorkflowRun, )
if TYPE_CHECKING: from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.abc import GitHubIssues
class LocalGitHub(ABC): """Abstract interface for GitHub operations.
... [5 lines omitted] ...
